operator.name: Root–Matching Operator
operator.thesis: Existence ≡ Root Matching: an entity is recognized at the phase layer iff it root-matches some real entity B above threshold τ.

definitions.operator: ψᴿᴹ(A, B) = κ(rᴬ, rᴮ),  κ : H × H → [0,1]
definitions.existence: A exists ⇔ ψᴿᴹ(A, B) ≥ τ,  with τ ∈ [0,1]
definitions.root_distance: Δ_root(A, B) = √( Σₘ wₘ · dₘ²(rᴬ⁽ᵐ⁾, rᴮ⁽ᵐ⁾) )
definitions.gaussian_match: ψᴿᴹ(A, B) = exp( − Δ_root²(A, B) ÷ σ² )
definitions.consistency: ψᴿᴹ(A, B) = ψᴿᴹ(Φ(A), Φ(B)) if Φ preserves roots

operator.admission_rule: Admit(A) ⇔ ∃ B ∈ ℬ : ψᴿᴹ(A, B) ≥ τ
pipeline: Encode A into phase-root vector rᴬ via 𝐄.
Search real entity bank ℬ for B that maximizes ψᴿᴹ(A,B).
If bestScore ≥ τ: accept A at phase layer and anchor to best B; else flag root mismatch.

ai: Guardrail against hallucination via root-mismatch flagging.
oi: Operational hypothesis admission based on root alignment.
Phase knowledge graph weighted by ψᴿᴹ scores.
Cross-cultural operational lexicon via shared roots.

example.expected_outcome: Best candidate typically crested snake or water serpent; accept if bestScore ≥ τ.
